name: vscode-hydra-room
description: A multi-head AI collaboration room with evidence-backed decisions, build/review handoffs, and native CLI workflows inside VS Code.

commands.title: Hydra: Send Codex Raw Terminal Line
Hydra: Poke Codex With Editor Context
Hydra: Poke Codex With Working Tree
Hydra: Send Claude Raw Terminal Line
Hydra: Poke Claude With Editor Context
Hydra: Poke Claude With Working Tree
Hydra: Poke Both With Editor Context
Hydra: Poke Both With Working Tree
Hydra: Advanced: Use Experimental Terminal Bridge
Hydra: Advanced: Run Terminal Bridge Self-Test
Hydra: Advanced: Run Claude Worker Fanout Smoke Test
Hydra: Advanced: Show Terminal Bridge Health
Hydra: Setup: Fix Codex CLI Path
Hydra: Setup: Fix Claude CLI Path
Hydra: Advanced: Use Safe One-Shot Transport

hydraRoom.transcriptPath.markdownDescription: Workspace-relative or absolute path where Hydra writes the Markdown transcript.

hydraRoom.workspaceRoot.markdownDescription: Advanced fallback project root for Extension Development Host sessions without an open VS Code workspace. Normal users should leave this blank and open a folder in VS Code.

hydraRoom.codexCommand.markdownDescription: Executable name or wrapper script for the OpenAI Codex CLI.

baseUrl.markdownDescription: openai-compatible: `https://` required unless the host is loopback or a private/`.local` address (local model servers).

apiKeyEnv.markdownDescription: NAME of the environment variable holding the API key. Never inline the key itself.

headers.markdownDescription: Extra HTTP headers. Reference secrets as `${env:NAME}`.

pricing.markdownDescription: Override per-model USD pricing (per million tokens) used for usage-rail cost estimates.

hydraRoom.agents.markdownDescription: Custom Hydra heads (open/local models). Each entry is an agent definition; user entries override built-ins by `id`. **Security:** trust-scoped — ignored in untrusted workspaces. API keys must be referenced by env-var name (`apiKeyEnv`), never inlined; definitions containing key-shaped values are rejected.

hydraRoom.roomRoster.markdownDescription: Ordered agent IDs active in this Hydra room. IDs must name built-in heads or entries in `hydraRoom.agents`. The first ID is the default opener and later IDs are eligible reactors/reviewers. Stored as a User setting because changing the roster changes which native agents Hydra can dispatch.

hydraRoom.codexExecArgsDiscussion.markdownDescription: Raw Codex CLI args for serialized discussion turns. `${workspaceFolder}` is expanded; the trailing `-` reads the Hydra prompt from stdin. Hydra also expands `${hydraPromptFile}`, `${hydraReplyFile}`, and `${hydraLogFile}` per request so native file/output flags can target Hydra artifacts.

hydraRoom.codexExecArgsBuild.markdownDescription: Raw Codex CLI args for Build. Defaults to workspace-write `codex exec`; add native flags such as config profiles, model, search, MCP/plugin-related config, sandbox changes, or Hydra request-file placeholders here.

hydraRoom.codexExecArgsReview.markdownDescription: Raw Codex CLI args for Review. Defaults to Codex's native review command over staged, unstaged, and untracked changes. Hydra does not downgrade these args by phase and expands Hydra request-file placeholders.

hydraRoom.codexDiscussionProfile.markdownDescription: Capability profile for Codex discussion turns. Defaults to `fullNative` for equal maximum Hydra authority; native tool implementations can still differ by CLI. Hydra still requires explicit per-workspace full-native consent. `custom` preserves `hydraRoom.codexExecArgsDiscussion`.

hydraRoom.codexBuildProfile.markdownDescription: Capability profile for Codex Build turns. Defaults to `fullNative` for equal maximum Hydra authority; native tool implementations can still differ by CLI. Hydra still requires explicit per-workspace full-native consent. `custom` preserves `hydraRoom.codexExecArgsBuild`.

hydraRoom.codexReviewProfile.markdownDescription: Capability profile for Codex Review turns. Defaults to `fullNative` for equal maximum Hydra authority; native tool implementations can still differ by CLI. Hydra still requires explicit per-workspace full-native consent. `custom` preserves `hydraRoom.codexExecArgsReview`.

hydraRoom.codexCaptureLastMessage.markdownDescription: When dispatching `codex exec`, automatically add Codex's native `--output-last-message` file capture and use that final answer in the room. This keeps stderr/plugin noise out of Hydra replies while preserving full diagnostics in `.hydra/agent-calls.jsonl`. Superseded by `hydraRoom.codexJson` when enabled (the JSON stream already carries the final agent message).

hydraRoom.codexJson.markdownDescription: When dispatching `codex exec` through safe one-shot transport, automatically request the typed event stream via `--json`, parse it through Hydra's `codexEvents` module, and use the final `agent_message` item as the reply in the room. Structured tool-call / command-execution / file-change events are summarized in `.hydra/agent-calls.jsonl`. Supersedes `hydraRoom.codexCaptureLastMessage` when both are enabled. Defaults to `true`: the parsed-event stream lets Hydra stream Codex's reply live (matching Claude's `claudeStreamJson`) and gives token-accurate Codex usage accounting. Set to `false` to fall back to plain one-shot text. The experimental terminal bridge always uses structured Codex output so the visible terminal does not echo the full prompt transcript.

hydraRoom.claudeCommand.markdownDescription: Executable name or wrapper script for the Claude Code CLI.

hydraRoom.claudeExecArgsDiscussion.markdownDescription: Raw Claude Code CLI args for serialized discussion turns. `${workspaceFolder}` is expanded. Hydra also expands `${hydraPromptFile}`, `${hydraReplyFile}`, and `${hydraLogFile}` per request so native file/output flags can target Hydra artifacts.

hydraRoom.claudeExecArgsBuild.markdownDescription: Raw Claude Code CLI args for Build. Defaults to print mode with edit-accepting permissions; add native flags such as --ide, --tools, --mcp-config, --plugin-dir, --agent, --settings, model, effort, or Hydra request-file placeholders here.

hydraRoom.claudeExecArgsReview.markdownDescription: Raw Claude Code CLI args for Review. Add native review/tool/MCP/plugin/model flags here. Hydra does not downgrade these args by phase and expands Hydra request-file placeholders.

hydraRoom.claudeDiscussionProfile.markdownDescription: Capability profile for Claude discussion turns. Defaults to `fullNative` for equal maximum Hydra authority; native tool implementations can still differ by CLI. Hydra still requires explicit per-workspace full-native consent. `custom` preserves `hydraRoom.claudeExecArgsDiscussion`.

hydraRoom.claudeBuildProfile.markdownDescription: Capability profile for Claude Build turns. Defaults to `fullNative` for equal maximum Hydra authority; native tool implementations can still differ by CLI. Hydra still requires explicit per-workspace full-native consent. `custom` preserves `hydraRoom.claudeExecArgsBuild`.

hydraRoom.claudeReviewProfile.markdownDescription: Capability profile for Claude Review turns. Defaults to `fullNative` for equal maximum Hydra authority; native tool implementations can still differ by CLI. Hydra still requires explicit per-workspace full-native consent. `custom` preserves `hydraRoom.claudeExecArgsReview`.

hydraRoom.claudeStreamJson.markdownDescription: When dispatching Claude print mode without an explicit output format, automatically request `--output-format stream-json --verbose`, parse the final assistant text for the room, and keep structured tool-event output in `.hydra/agent-calls.jsonl`.

hydraRoom.claudeStreamJsonIncludePartial.markdownDescription: When `hydraRoom.claudeStreamJson` is enabled, also request Claude's partial message stream so Hydra can summarize tool-use and task lifecycle events in `.hydra/agent-calls.jsonl`.

hydraRoom.claudeDebugFile.markdownDescription: When `hydraRoom.claudeStreamJson` is enabled, pass Claude `--debug-file ${hydraLogFile}` so raw native debug output is captured in the per-request Hydra log artifact.

hydraRoom.geminiCommand.markdownDescription: Executable name or wrapper script for the Google Gemini CLI.

hydraRoom.geminiExecArgsDiscussion.markdownDescription: Raw Gemini CLI args for serialized discussion turns. `${workspaceFolder}` is expanded; the trailing `-` reads the Hydra prompt from stdin. Hydra also expands `${hydraPromptFile}`, `${hydraReplyFile}`, and `${hydraLogFile}` per request so native file/output flags can target Hydra artifacts. Placeholder pending a verified real Gemini CLI install (tracked follow-up).

hydraRoom.geminiExecArgsBuild.markdownDescription: Raw Gemini CLI args for Build. Add native flags such as model, sandbox/approval mode, or Hydra request-file placeholders here. Placeholder pending a verified real Gemini CLI install (tracked follow-up).

hydraRoom.geminiExecArgsReview.markdownDescription: Raw Gemini CLI args for Review. Hydra does not downgrade these args by phase and expands Hydra request-file placeholders. Placeholder pending a verified real Gemini CLI install (tracked follow-up).

hydraRoom.manyHeadsMode.markdownDescription: Claude Worker Fanout (experimental; legacy key name): mirror each structured agent stream into a shared `.hydra/live/<turn>/<agent>.jsonl` channel so Codex can inspect Claude-side work while it is still streaming. In one-shot parallel discussion turns, Hydra launches `hydraRoom.manyHeadsClaudeWorkerCount` local subscription-backed Claude workers. These workers are not independent Hydra head identities. Stored as a User setting and forced off in untrusted workspaces.

hydraRoom.agentInitiatedDuels.markdownDescription: Allow a reactor or closer head to initiate a rated formal duel from its own reply. Hydra policy validates and admits the challenge, then runs both sealed full-native commitments automatically. The human remains the independent adjudicator. Stored as a User setting and forced off in untrusted workspaces.

hydraRoom.manyHeadsClaudeWorkerCount.markdownDescription: Number of local subscription-backed Claude workers to launch for Claude Worker Fanout parallel discussion turns. This multiplies official Claude Code / Agent SDK dispatches and therefore Agent SDK credit usage; workspace overrides are ignored in untrusted workspaces.

hydraRoom.nativePathPrepend.markdownDescription: Directories prepended to PATH/Path for every native Codex and Claude dispatch, in both one-shot and terminal-bridge transports. Supports `${workspaceFolder}` and `${env:NAME}` placeholders.

hydraRoom.nativeEnv.markdownDescription: Environment variables applied to every native Codex and Claude dispatch, in both one-shot and terminal-bridge transports. Values support `${workspaceFolder}` and `${env:NAME}` placeholders.

hydraRoom.codexNativePathPrepend.markdownDescription: Codex-only directories appended after `hydraRoom.nativePathPrepend` when constructing PATH/Path for native Codex dispatches. Supports `${workspaceFolder}` and `${env:NAME}` placeholders.

hydraRoom.codexNativeEnv.markdownDescription: Codex-only environment variables layered over `hydraRoom.nativeEnv` for native Codex dispatches. Values support `${workspaceFolder}` and `${env:NAME}` placeholders.

hydraRoom.claudeNativePathPrepend.markdownDescription: Claude-only directories appended after `hydraRoom.nativePathPrepend` when constructing PATH/Path for native Claude dispatches. Supports `${workspaceFolder}` and `${env:NAME}` placeholders.

hydraRoom.claudeNativeEnv.markdownDescription: Claude-only environment variables layered over `hydraRoom.nativeEnv` for native Claude dispatches. Values support `${workspaceFolder}` and `${env:NAME}` placeholders.

hydraRoom.geminiNativePathPrepend.markdownDescription: Gemini-only directories appended after `hydraRoom.nativePathPrepend` when constructing PATH/Path for native Gemini dispatches. Supports `${workspaceFolder}` and `${env:NAME}` placeholders.

hydraRoom.geminiNativeEnv.markdownDescription: Gemini-only environment variables layered over `hydraRoom.nativeEnv` for native Gemini dispatches. Values support `${workspaceFolder}` and `${env:NAME}` placeholders.

hydraRoom.transport.markdownDescription: Deprecated. Hydra transport is session-only and always starts in safe one-shot mode. Use the in-room advanced button or commands to temporarily enable the experimental terminal bridge.

hydraRoom.autopilotOnStart.markdownDescription: Run Hydra Autopilot when the room opens: Doctor checks, terminal bridge self-test, and automatic transport selection.

hydraRoom.preferTerminalBridgeOnStart.markdownDescription: When Autopilot passes the terminal bridge self-test, automatically use the visible native terminal bridge instead of safe one-shot transport. Stored as a User setting and forced off in untrusted workspaces because the terminal bridge is experimental.

hydraRoom.codexTerminalCommand.markdownDescription: Optional startup command sent once when Hydra opens the Codex terminal. Leave blank for command-dispatch mode. `${workspaceFolder}` is expanded.

hydraRoom.claudeTerminalCommand.markdownDescription: Optional startup command sent once when Hydra opens the Claude terminal. Leave blank for command-dispatch mode. `${workspaceFolder}` is expanded.

hydraRoom.terminalStartupDelayMs.markdownDescription: Maximum milliseconds to wait for the visible native terminal startup probe after running an agent terminal startup command.

hydraRoom.terminalReplyPollMs.markdownDescription: Polling interval for reply files in VS Code's private per-workspace extension storage when using `terminalBridge` transport.

hydraRoom.terminalBridgeTimeoutMs.markdownDescription: Maximum time to wait for terminal bridge self-tests and setup checks. Normal agent calls use the Discussion or Build/Review timeout settings.

anyOf.description: Character cap applied to every phase. Set to 0 to include the loaded instruction context without an additional prompt-time cap.
Per-phase repository-instruction caps. Discussion covers opener, reactor, closer, and parallel discussion turns.

hydraRoom.oneShotWorkspaceInstructionsMa.markdownDescription: Maximum characters of repository instruction files injected into one-shot Hydra prompts after filtering out the recipient CLI's own native instruction files. Set a single number for every phase, or use `{ "discussion": 12000, "build": 12000, "review": 12000 }`. A value of 0 includes the loaded instruction context without an additional prompt-time cap for that phase.

hydraRoom.terminalBridgeWorkspaceInstruc.markdownDescription: Maximum characters of repository instruction files injected into visible native-terminal prompts after filtering out the recipient CLI's own native instruction files. Set to 0 to skip Hydra-injected repository instructions in terminal bridge prompts.

anyOf.description: Character cap applied to every phase. Set to 0 to keep the full active transcript and disable threshold refreshes.
Per-phase transcript caps. Discussion covers opener, reactor, closer, and parallel discussion turns.

hydraRoom.promptTranscriptMaxChars.markdownDescription: Maximum characters of active room transcript injected into agent prompts after objective, workspace instructions, and wiki context. This is a per-call cost and attention guardrail, not a model context-window limit. Hydra keeps the newest transcript messages and adds an omission notice when older active history is trimmed. Either set one number for every phase, or use `{ "discussion": 80000, "build": 400000, "review": 400000 }`. The wiki context refresh threshold uses the largest configured cap, so the default refreshes at the 400k durable-memory boundary while discussion prompts stay lean.

hydraRoom.editorContextMaxChars.markdownDescription: Maximum characters from the active editor selection or file attached to direct native terminal pokes.

hydraRoom.attachmentPreviewMaxChars.markdownDescription: Maximum text-preview characters injected into a room turn for each uploaded file. Hydra still copies the full file into `.hydra/attachments/` for the agents to inspect directly. Set to 0 to attach paths without inline previews.

hydraRoom.attachmentMaxBytes.markdownDescription: Maximum bytes allowed for a single file attached to a room turn. Defaults to 10 MiB.

hydraRoom.attachmentTotalMaxBytes.markdownDescription: Maximum total bytes allowed across pending room attachments. Defaults to 32 MiB.

hydraRoom.wikiContextMaxChars.markdownDescription: Maximum characters of compiled `.hydra/wiki` context injected into agent prompts. Set to 0 to disable wiki prompt context.

hydraRoom.wikiPromptIncludeLog.markdownDescription: Include append-only `.hydra/wiki/log.md` entries in every agent prompt. Disabled by default so routine prompts spend wiki budget on compiled `context.md` and `index.md`; wrapups still read the log tail for maintenance.

hydraRoom.wikiWrapupEnabled.markdownDescription: After a successful discussion turn, ask one native agent to distill durable facts into `.hydra/wiki/context.md`, update `.hydra/wiki/index.md` when useful, append `.hydra/wiki/log.md`, and save a raw source snapshot under `.hydra/wiki/raw/turns/` when the wiki changes. Disable to keep the wiki manual-only.

hydraRoom.wikiWrapupAgent.markdownDescription: Agent used for automatic wiki wrapups. `auto` chooses the lower estimated configured discussion-model cost from `hydraRoom.modelPrices`; `codex` or `claude` pins the maintainer.

hydraRoom.wikiWrapupMaxSourceChars.markdownDescription: Maximum characters from the just-finished room turn sent to the automatic wiki wrapup prompt.

hydraRoom.wikiWrapupTimeoutMs.markdownDescription: Maximum runtime in milliseconds for the background automatic wiki wrapup agent call.

hydraRoom.wikiRawTurnsKeepDays.markdownDescription: Days of immutable `.hydra/wiki/raw/turns/` snapshots to keep after automatic wiki wrapups. Set to 0 to keep raw turn snapshots indefinitely.

hydraRoom.promptBodyRetentionDays.markdownDescription: Days to keep full rendered prompt bodies in `.hydra/prompts/index.jsonl` before `Hydra: Clean Workspace State` compacts them to metadata-only records. Set to 0 to compact prompt bodies whenever cleanup runs.

hydraRoom.diagnosticRetentionDays.markdownDescription: Days to keep copied attachments and legacy workspace-local terminal diagnostics before `Hydra: Clean Workspace State` deletes them. Current terminal-bridge artifacts live in VS Code extension storage and use a short automatic retention sweep. `.hydra/prompts/index.jsonl` is preserved and compacted separately.

hydraRoom.firstSpeaker.markdownDescription: Default opener for serialized discussion turns. The webview can flip this per turn without changing the setting.

hydraRoom.discussionMode.markdownDescription: How normal room messages run Discussion. `parallelOnBoth` preserves the default behavior: serialized opener -> reactor -> optional closer unless the user explicitly addresses both agents. `serial` always uses the serialized loop. `parallel` always dispatches Codex and Claude at the same time and returns after both replies finish, trading less critique choreography for lower wall-clock latency.

hydraRoom.oneShotTimeoutMs.markdownDescription: Maximum runtime per Build or Review agent call in milliseconds. Set to `0` to disable the wall-clock cap and rely on Stop Current Turn.

hydraRoom.discussionTimeoutMs.markdownDescription: Maximum runtime per Discussion opener, reactor, closer, or parallel call in milliseconds. Set to `0` to disable the wall-clock cap and rely on Stop Current Turn.

hydraRoom.diffMaxLines.markdownDescription: Soft cap for `git diff` output before truncation in the Review prompt.

hydraRoom.verifyCommand.markdownDescription: Shell command Hydra runs for one-click verification. Leave blank to infer from package.json scripts in this order: `verify:fast`, `verify`, `check && test`, `test`, `check`, then `lint`. Inference is gated by Workspace Trust; untrusted workspaces refuse inferred package.json commands.

hydraRoom.verifyTimeoutMs.markdownDescription: Maximum runtime for Hydra verification commands in milliseconds.

hydraRoom.verifyMaxOutputChars.markdownDescription: Maximum stdout/stderr characters retained from each verification run.

hydraRoom.autoVerifyAfterBuild.markdownDescription: Automatically run Hydra verification after a successful Build phase.

hydraRoom.autoScorePassingBuilds.markdownDescription: Automatically add deterministic passive-score evidence when a serial builder changes the Git-visible workspace and Hydra verifies that exact post-build state under an unchanged eligible package-script plan and bounded verifier-control inventory captured before dispatch. Eligible package-manager executables are resolved outside the workspace; repeated receipts under one plan/control regime share a correlated round. Unsupported/dynamic commands may still verify but do not score. No-op, parallel, failed, cancelled, timed-out, unconfirmed, control-changing, or state-changing runs are never scored. Standings never change agent authority.

hydraRoom.autoAdvanceActionableDefaults.markdownDescription: Automatically execute actionable Decision Packet defaults when they have no blockers and require no user decision. For example, a discussion default that names a builder enters Build without requiring the user to click Accept Default. Stored as a User setting and forced off in untrusted workspaces.

hydraRoom.autoSkipCloserOnAgreement.markdownDescription: Skip the discussion closer when the reactor begins with `Agree:` and its Decision Packet has `Decision needed from user: none` and `Blockers: none`. Disable to force the traditional opener/reactor/closer cadence.

hydraRoom.autoAdvanceSendInstructionMaxC.markdownDescription: Maximum number of consecutive auto-advanced send-instruction turns before Hydra pauses and waits for Accept Default. Prevents runaway discussion loops when agents keep emitting non-blocking free-form defaults. Phase-changing auto-advances (assign builder, request review, hand back) reset the counter.

hydraRoom.sessionCostCapUsd.markdownDescription: If greater than 0, Hydra pauses auto-advance once the session's estimated cost reaches this USD value. Cost is computed from `.hydra/usage.jsonl` using `hydraRoom.modelPrices`. Set to 0 to disable. Useful guard rail when running autonomous loops. Note: on the default Codex one-shot path (`hydraRoom.codexJson: false`) there is no per-token usage breakdown, so Hydra estimates conservatively by billing the whole plain-text reply at the output rate — the displayed figure (and therefore when this cap trips) runs high. Enable `hydraRoom.codexJson` for token-accurate Codex accounting.

enumDescriptions: Never warn or block on Claude automation spend.
Surface a warning once monthly Claude automation spend reaches the cap.
Block Claude Worker Fanout over the cap; single Claude turns still warn.
Block all Claude automation turns once monthly spend reaches the cap.
hydraRoom.claudeAutomationCreditGuard.markdownDescription: How Hydra reacts when **subscription-backed** Claude automation spend reaches `hydraRoom.claudeAgentCreditCapUsd` in the current calendar month. As of 2026-06-15, subscription `claude -p` / Agent SDK usage draws from a capped monthly Agent SDK credit pool instead of pay-as-you-go API billing, so heavy Hydra automation can exhaust it. Only applies when Claude is authenticated via a subscription (claude.ai / a plan); API-key auth is pay-as-you-go and is never warned or blocked. Spend is summed from `.hydra/usage.jsonl` (Claude rows only). This setting only affects Hydra's own cost/decision logic — it injects into no CLI spawn — so it is window/resource scoped, not `application`.

hydraRoom.claudeAgentCreditCapUsd.markdownDescription: Monthly Claude automation spend ceiling in USD that `hydraRoom.claudeAutomationCreditGuard` checks against. Defaults to `200` (the Max 20x / eligible Enterprise Premium Agent SDK monthly credit). Set to `0` to disable the threshold. Spend is summed per UTC calendar month from Claude rows in `.hydra/usage.jsonl`; prefer native `total_cost_usd` when Claude stream-json is enabled.

hydraRoom.claudeAgentEstimatedRunCostUsd.markdownDescription: Estimated USD reservation Hydra adds before dispatching each Claude automation call when evaluating `hydraRoom.claudeAutomationCreditGuard`. Defaults to `$1` so Claude Worker Fanout cannot launch many workers against the same stale recorded spend. Set to `0` to disable in-flight projection. This only affects Hydra's own cost/decision logic and is not trust-scoped.

hydraRoom.handoffWebhookUrl.markdownDescription: Optional HTTPS endpoint Hydra POSTs to when a new decision packet has a non-empty `Decision needed from user` field. Body is `{ event: "hydra.decision_needed", timestamp, workspace, agent, phase, decisionNeededFromUser, defaultNextAction, recommendation, blockers, objective }`. Use a Slack incoming webhook URL or your own paging service. For Telegram, use `hydraRoom.telegramBotToken` + `hydraRoom.telegramChatId` instead — Hydra speaks the Telegram Bot API directly. Dedupes per packet timestamp.

hydraRoom.telegramBotToken.markdownDescription: Telegram Bot API token (the long `123456:ABC-DEF…` string from BotFather). Empty disables Telegram. **Treat as secret** — prefer User Settings over Workspace Settings so it doesn't get committed. Alternatively set the `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` environment variable and leave this blank.

hydraRoom.telegramChatId.markdownDescription: Telegram chat id Hydra sends notifications to. Your personal chat with the bot, a group, or a channel. Find it by messaging your bot and visiting `https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/getUpdates`. Required for Telegram notifications to fire. **Security:** when inbound polling is on, authorization is chat-level — pointing this at a **group** lets *every member of that group* (and anyone who obtains its invite link) drive agent turns in your workspace. Prefer your private 1:1 chat with the bot, or set `hydraRoom.telegramInboundAllowedSenderIds` to gate individual senders.

hydraRoom.telegramNotifyOnDecisionNeeded.markdownDescription: Send a Telegram message when a decision packet's `Decision needed from user` is non-empty. Requires `hydraRoom.telegramBotToken` and `hydraRoom.telegramChatId`.

hydraRoom.telegramInboundPollingEnabled.markdownDescription: Poll Telegram Bot API updates and inject accepted messages into the Hydra room as user input. Requires `hydraRoom.telegramBotToken`, `hydraRoom.telegramChatId`, and the `hydraRoom.telegramInboundCommandPrefix` prefix. Disabled by default because inbound Telegram messages are remote untrusted input. **Authorization is chat-level, not per-sender:** anyone who can post to the configured `hydraRoom.telegramChatId` can drive agent turns under the configured execution permissions — keep that chat private (a 1:1 with the bot, or a group of fully-trusted members).

hydraRoom.telegramInboundCommandPrefix.markdownDescription: Required prefix for Telegram inbound polling. Messages from the configured chat must start with this prefix (for example `/hydra continue with the default`) before Hydra treats them as room input. Set to an empty string only for a private bot chat you fully trust.

hydraRoom.telegramInboundAllowedSenderId.markdownDescription: Optional per-sender allowlist for Telegram inbound polling. Empty (the default) means every sender in the configured `hydraRoom.telegramChatId` may drive agent turns — for a group chat, that is every member. Set it to a list of Telegram numeric user ids (as strings, e.g. `["123456789"]`) to accept inbound commands ONLY from those senders; every other sender in the chat is silently ignored. Find an id by messaging `@userinfobot`, or by reading `from.id` from `https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/getUpdates`. Requires `hydraRoom.telegramInboundPollingEnabled`.

hydraRoom.telegramInboundPollIntervalSec.markdownDescription: How often Hydra polls Telegram for inbound commands when `hydraRoom.telegramInboundPollingEnabled` is enabled.

hydraRoom.promptTemplates.markdownDescription: Saved composer templates. Run `Hydra: Insert Prompt Template` (or its keybinding `Ctrl+Alt+T`) to pick one; the body replaces the current composer text. Example: `[{ "name": "Survey before code", "body": "Survey the affected files first. List what you'd change before making any edits." }]`.

anyOf.description: Model applied to every Claude phase. Empty = CLI default.
Per-phase models for Claude. Missing phases fall back to the CLI default.

hydraRoom.claudeModel.markdownDescription: Model passed to Claude Code CLI as `--model <id>` on every dispatch. Either a single string (applied to every phase) or an object with per-phase overrides like `{ "discussion": "sonnet", "build": "opus", "review": "fable" }`. Aliases (`fable`, `sonnet`, `opus`, `haiku`) always resolve to the current build of that family; or pin a full ID (`claude-fable-5`, `claude-sonnet-5`, `claude-opus-4-8`, `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`). Use `Hydra: Choose Model` (Ctrl+Alt+M) to change live.

anyOf.description: Model applied to every Codex phase. Empty = CLI default.
Per-phase models for Codex. Missing phases fall back to the CLI default.

hydraRoom.codexModel.markdownDescription: Model passed to Codex CLI as `--model <id>` on every `exec` dispatch. Either a single string (applied to every phase) or an object with per-phase overrides. Current Codex model IDs include `gpt-5.6-sol` (`gpt-5.6` alias), `gpt-5.6-terra`, `gpt-5.6-luna`, `gpt-5.5`, `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5.4-mini`, `gpt-5.3-codex`, `gpt-5.3-codex-spark` (interactive only, no API), and `gpt-5.2`. Run `codex debug models` (or `Hydra: Refresh Codex Models`) to inspect what your install actually exposes. Use `Hydra: Choose Model` (Ctrl+Alt+M) to change live.

anyOf.description: Model applied to every Gemini phase. Empty = CLI default.
Per-phase models for Gemini. Missing phases fall back to the CLI default.

hydraRoom.geminiModel.markdownDescription: Model passed to the Gemini CLI as `--model <id>`. Either a single string (applied to every phase) or an object with per-phase overrides like `{ "discussion": "gemini-2.5-flash", "build": "gemini-2.5-pro", "review": "gemini-2.5-pro" }`.

hydraRoom.claudeEffort.markdownDescription: Reasoning/effort level passed to Claude as `--effort <level>` on every dispatch. Either a single string (applied to every phase) or an object with per-phase overrides like `{ "discussion": "low", "build": "high" }`. Levels: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `xhigh`, `max`. Leave blank to use the CLI's default. If you've already added `--effort` to `hydraRoom.claudeExecArgs*`, the explicit arg wins. Use `Hydra: Choose Thinking Level` (Ctrl+Alt+E).

hydraRoom.codexReasoning.markdownDescription: Reasoning effort passed to Codex as `-c model_reasoning_effort=<level>` on every `exec` dispatch. Either a single string or per-phase object. Levels can vary by model/runtime; common values include `none`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, `xhigh`, `max`, and `ultra`. Run `Hydra: Refresh Codex Models` then check the chooser description for the model's `default_reasoning_level`. Leave blank to use the CLI's default. If `-c model_reasoning_effort=` is already in `hydraRoom.codexExecArgs*`, the explicit arg wins. Use `Hydra: Choose Thinking Level` (Ctrl+Alt+E).

hydraRoom.modelPrices.markdownDescription: Per-agent token pricing for the session cost meter, USD per **million** tokens. The keys `claude` and `codex` set each agent's default price; any other key is treated as a per-model override matched by model id (lower-cased), for example `"gpt-5.5"` or `"opus"`. Each value object accepts `inputPerMTok`, `outputPerMTok`, `cacheReadPerMTok`, and `cacheCreatePerMTok`, all non-negative numbers. Omitted fields fall back to Hydra's built-in defaults (Sonnet 4.6 pricing for Claude, a GPT-5-family blend for Codex). Example: `{ "claude": { "inputPerMTok": 3, "outputPerMTok": 15, "cacheReadPerMTok": 0.3, "cacheCreatePerMTok": 3.75 }, "codex": { "inputPerMTok": 1.25, "outputPerMTok": 10 } }`.

hydraRoom.autoRequestReviewAfterPassingV.markdownDescription: Automatically request Review after an automatic post-build verification passes. Off by default so users can inspect build output first.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Hydra spawns Codex and Claude with workspace-write authority and runs a configured verify command. In untrusted workspaces, Hydra ignores workspace-scoped settings for CLI paths, args, env, terminal startup, verify command, automatic execution controls, handoff webhook, and Telegram credentials so a hostile repo cannot redirect or auto-start those sinks.

scripts.test: pnpm run clean && pnpm run test:fast
scripts.test:fast: tsc --incremental -p ./ && pnpm run copy-fixtures && pnpm run setup-vscode-stub && node --test --test-concurrency=4 dist/test/*.test.js
